name: Centor Score (Modified/McIsaac)
description: Estimates probability that pharyngitis is streptococcal, and suggests management course.
when_to_use: Patients with recent onset (≤3 days) acute pharyngitis
Primarly children with pharyngitis. The risk decreases significantly with age into adulthood
when_not_to_use: Patients with symptom duration longer than 3 days
Patients without recent onset acute pharyngitis

interpretation.description: 1-2.5% probability of strep pharyngitis. No further testing or antibiotics.
5-10% probability of strep pharyngitis. No further testing or antibiotics.
11-17% probability of strep pharyngitis. Optional rapid strep testing and/or culture.
28-35% probability of strep pharyngitis. Consider rapid strep testing and/or culture.
51-53% probability of strep pharyngitis. Consider rapid strep testing and/or culture. Empiric antibiotics may be appropriate depending on the specific scenario.

references.citation: McIsaac WJ, White D, Tannenbaum D, Low DE. A clinical score to reduce unnecessary antibiotic use in patients with sore throat. CMAJ. 1998 Jan 13;158(1):75-83.

notes: Use only in patients with recent onset (≤3 days) acute pharyngitis. Strep pharyngitis is incredibly rare in patients under 3. 2012 IDSA guidelines no longer recommend empiric treatment alone; they recommend testing patients at higher risk for strep pharyngitis, but not giving antibiotics until a rapid test or throat culture is positive. A score of -1 is possible if the patient is ≥45 years with no other criteria present.
